description: REFUSED. Worst proposed artist node ever — or, actually, not worst, just unnecessary. Yusuke Ohmura has 7 corpus credits, all spelled consistently as 'Yusuke Ohmura' across every print, zero alias drift. The artist layer's stated purpose per bbl-artists-layer-built is alias disambiguation — Jenn Ravenna Tran exists because Scryfall stored 'Ravenna Tran' on Dominaria-era prints while the printed card credit was 'Jenn Ravenna Tran' / 'Jenn Ravenna,' and the artist_resolve.py helper maps the stored credit to the canonical form at render time. Yusuke Ohmura needs none of this work — the resolver passes through the stored credit unchanged, and there is no canonical-versus-stored divergence to disambiguate. A portfolio-coherence node would carry Discrete Lair value (all 7 credits are trainer / supporter human-portrait cards, a real signature) but that is bundler logic, not edge-graph logic. Edgelord and Mr. Nodeley both decline. The wave-48 refused-with-receipts sign-off pattern carries forward.
refusal_reason: Artist nodes serve alias disambiguation; Yusuke Ohmura has zero alias drift across 7 corpus credits. The portfolio-thematic-coherence case (all human-portrait trainer / supporter cards) is real but is bundler-domain not graph-domain and does not require a node. If the bundler ever wants 'all Ohmura-painted trainers' as a Discrete Lair candidacy, the artist field already supports that query without a node.
future_trigger_conditions: If a future Collectr CSV import introduces a Yusuke Ohmura credit with a different spelling (Ōmura, Omura, Yusuke Ōmura, or similar), revisit and create the node as an alias-disambiguation entry
If the bundler workflow specifies that portfolio-thematic-coherence nodes are useful for query, revisit and create as a Discrete Lair candidacy anchor
If a future Discrete Lair specifically targets 'all Ohmura-painted Pokemon trainers,' the lair itself can reference the artist field directly without a node
